Whoever by faith chooses God as their protector will find in him all that they need or can desire. Those who have found comfort in making the Lord their refuge cannot help but want others to do so. Spiritual life is protected by Divine grace from Satan’s temptations, which are like a hunter's snares, and from the contagion of sin, which is a terrible plague. Great security is promised to believers even in danger. Wisdom will keep them from being afraid without cause, and faith will keep them from being unduly afraid. Whatever happens, our heavenly Father’s will is done; and we have no reason to fear. God's people will see not only God's promises fulfilled but his threatenings as well. Then let sinners come to the Lord upon his mercy-seat through the Redeemer's name, and encourage others to trust in him also.

Whatever happens, nothing will hurt the believer; though trouble and affliction befall, it will come not for their hurt, but for good, even if at present it is not joyous but grievous. Those who truly know God will set their love upon him. They constantly call upon him through prayer. His promise is that he will in due time deliver the believer out of trouble, and in the meantime be with them in trouble. The Lord will manage all his worldly concerns and preserve his life on earth as long as it is good for him. For encouragement in this, he looks to Jesus. He will live long enough until he has done the work he was sent into this world for and is ready for heaven. Who would wish to live a day longer than God has some work to do either by them or upon them? A man may die young yet be satisfied with living. But a wicked person is not satisfied even with long life. At length, the believer’s conflict ends; he has done forever with trouble, sin, and temptation.
